documents.title: Option: oci.credentialsLeak
documents.content: Options for credential leak detection in container images.

documents.title: Option: oci.credentialsLeak.configPath
documents.content: Path where global credentials leak check configuration files will be stored.

documents.title: Option: oci.credentialsLeak.trivy
documents.content: Configuration for detecting credentials leaks using Trivy.

documents.title: Option: oci.credentialsLeak.trivy.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable credentials leak detection with Trivy.

documents.title: Option: oci.cve
documents.content: Configuration for Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) scanning in container images.

documents.title: Option: oci.cve.configPath
documents.content: Path where CVE scanner configuration files will be stored.

documents.title: Option: oci.cve.grype
documents.content: Configuration for CVE scanning using Grype.

documents.title: Option: oci.cve.grype.config
documents.content: Configuration for Grype scanner settings.

documents.title: Option: oci.cve.grype.config.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable Grype configuration file generation.

documents.title: Option: oci.cve.grype.config.rootPath
documents.content: Path where Grype configuration files will be stored.

documents.title: Option: oci.cve.grype.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable CVE scanning with Grype.

documents.title: Option: oci.cve.trivy
documents.content: Configuration for CVE scanning using Trivy.

documents.title: Option: oci.cve.trivy.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable CVE scanning with Trivy.

documents.title: Option: oci.cve.trivy.ignore
documents.content: Configuration for CVE exclusions in Trivy scans.

documents.title: Option: oci.cve.trivy.ignore.extra
documents.content: Additional CVE identifiers to ignore globally in Trivy scans.

documents.title: Option: oci.cve.trivy.ignore.fileEnabled
documents.content: Whether to enable Trivy CVE ignore file generation.

documents.title: Option: oci.cve.trivy.ignore.rootPath
documents.content: Path where Trivy CVE ignore files will be stored.

documents.title: Option: oci.cve.vulnix
documents.content: Configuration for Nix-native CVE scanning using vulnix.

documents.title: Option: oci.cve.vulnix.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable CVE scanning with vulnix.

documents.title: Option: oci.cve.vulnix.whitelist
documents.content: Configuration for vulnix CVE whitelist.

documents.title: Option: oci.cve.vulnix.whitelist.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable vulnix whitelist file.

documents.title: Option: oci.cve.vulnix.whitelist.rootPath
documents.content: Path where vulnix whitelist files will be stored.

documents.title: Option: oci.devShellPackage
documents.content: The package to use for the development shell.

documents.title: Option: oci.enableDevShell
documents.content: Whether to enable the flake development shell.

documents.title: Option: oci.enableFlakeOutputs
documents.content: Whether to automatically expose OCI apps, packages, and checks as flake outputs.

documents.title: Option: oci.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable Enable the OCI module..

documents.title: Option: oci.fromImageManifestRootPath
documents.content: The root path to store the pulled OCI image manifest JSON lockfiles.

documents.title: Option: oci.registry
documents.content: The OCI registry to use for pushing and pulling images.

documents.title: Option: oci.rootPath
documents.content: The root path to store the Nix OCI resources.

documents.title: Option: oci.sbom
documents.content: Configuration for Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) generation in container images.

documents.title: Option: oci.sbom.path
documents.content: Path where SBOM files will be stored.

documents.title: Option: oci.sbom.syft
documents.content: Configuration for SBOM generation using Syft.

documents.title: Option: oci.sbom.syft.config
documents.content: Configuration settings for Syft SBOM generation.

documents.title: Option: oci.sbom.syft.config.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable Syft configuration file generation.

documents.title: Option: oci.sbom.syft.config.rootPath
documents.content: Path where Syft configuration files will be stored.

documents.title: Option: oci.sbom.syft.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable SBOM generation with Syft.

documents.title: Option: oci.signing
documents.content: Configuration for OCI image signing using cosign / Sigstore.

documents.title: Option: oci.signing.cosign
documents.content: Configuration for image signing with cosign.

documents.title: Option: oci.signing.cosign.annotations
documents.content: Key-value annotations to attach to every cosign signature. These appear in cosign verify output and can be used for policy enforcement (e.g. with Kyverno or OPA).

documents.title: Option: oci.signing.cosign.certificateIdentityRegexp
documents.content: Regular expression to match the certificate identity when verifying keyless signatures. Required for keyless verification. Example: "https://github.com/myorg/.*" or an email pattern.

documents.title: Option: oci.signing.cosign.certificateOidcIssuerRegexp
documents.content: Regular expression to match the OIDC issuer when verifying keyless signatures. Required for keyless verification. Example: "https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com".

documents.title: Option: oci.signing.cosign.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable OCI image signing with cosign.

documents.title: Option: oci.signing.cosign.key
documents.content:
Path or KMS URI for the cosign signing key. Supports local files, environment variables, and KMS URIs:
Local file: ./cosign.key
Environment variable: env://COSIGN_PRIVATE_KEY
AWS KMS: awskms://[ENDPOINT]/[ID/ALIAS/ARN]
GCP KMS: gcpkms://projects/[PROJECT]/locations/[LOC]/keyRings/[RING]/cryptoKeys/[KEY]
Azure Key Vault: azurekms://[VAULT_NAME][VAULT_URI]/[KEY]
HashCorp Vault: hashivault://[KEY] Only used when keyless is false.

documents.title: Option: oci.signing.cosign.keyless
documents.content: Use keyless (OIDC) signing via Sigstore Fulcio. When true, cosign authenticates via an OIDC provider (GitHub Actions, Google, Microsoft) and issues ephemeral certificates. No key management required. When false, key must be set.

documents.title: Option: oci.signing.cosign.verify
documents.content: Whether to verify the signature immediately after signing.

documents.title: Option: oci.test
documents.content: Global configuration for container testing tools.

documents.title: Option: oci.test.containerStructureTest
documents.content: Configuration for container-structure-test validation tool.

documents.title: Option: oci.test.containerStructureTest.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable container-structure-test globally for all containers.

documents.title: Option: oci.test.dgoss
documents.content: Configuration for dgoss (Docker + goss) testing framework.

documents.title: Option: oci.test.dgoss.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable dgoss testing globally for all containers.

documents.title: Option: oci.test.dgoss.hermetic
documents.content: Run dgoss as a pure Nix derivation (check) using podman inside the Nix sandbox. Requires extra-sandbox-paths = /sys/fs/cgroup in nix.conf.

documents.title: Option: oci.test.dive
documents.content: Configuration for Dive container image analysis tool.

documents.title: Option: oci.test.dive.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable Dive analysis globally for all containers.

documents.title: Option: oci.containers
documents.content: OCI containers to build, load, and optionally run. Each entry builds an image via nix2container and creates a systemd service to load it into the container runtime.

documents.title: Option: oci.debug
documents.content: Add debug build in output.

documents.title: Option: oci.debug.entrypoint
documents.content: Debug entrypoint wrapper configuration.

documents.title: Option: oci.debug.entrypoint.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable debug entrypoint wrapper.

documents.title: Option: oci.debug.entrypoint.wrapper
documents.content: Default behavior run sleep infinity fallback if entrypoint fail.

documents.title: Option: oci.flake.apps
documents.content: OCI-related apps that can be exposed as flake outputs.

documents.title: Option: oci.flake.checks
documents.content: OCI-related checks that can be exposed as flake outputs.

documents.title: Option: oci.flake.packages
documents.content: OCI container packages that can be exposed as flake outputs.

documents.title: Option: oci.packages.containerStructureTest
documents.content: The package to use for container-structure-test.

documents.title: Option: oci.packages.cosign
documents.content: The package to use for cosign.

documents.title: Option: oci.packages.dgoss
documents.content: The package to use for dgoss.

documents.title: Option: oci.packages.dive
documents.content: The package to use for dive.

documents.title: Option: oci.packages.grype
documents.content: The package to use for grype.

documents.title: Option: oci.packages.nix2container
documents.content: The nix2container package.

documents.title: Option: oci.packages.podman
documents.content: The package to use for podman.

documents.title: Option: oci.packages.regctl
documents.content: The package to use for regctl (multi-arch manifest tool).

documents.title: Option: oci.packages.skaffold
documents.content: The package to use for skaffold.

documents.title: Option: oci.packages.skopeo
documents.content: The package to use for skopeo.

documents.title: Option: oci.packages.syft
documents.content: The package to use for syft.

documents.title: Option: oci.packages.trivy
documents.content: The package to use for trivy.

documents.title: Option: oci.packages.vulnix
documents.content: The package to use for vulnix.

documents.title: Option: oci.perContainer
documents.content:
Per-container module definition.
Multiple modules can contribute to this option. Each contribution is a module that will be evaluated for every container with container-specific context.
The module receives these special arguments:
name: the attribute name of the container (from types.attrsOf)
config: the container's config (for reading within the module)
globalConfig: the top-level flake config
perSystemConfig: the perSystem config
system: the current system
pkgs: nixpkgs for current system
lib: nixpkgs lib

documents.title: Option: configFiles
documents.content:
Config file derivations to emplace in the container image.
Each entry should be a derivation producing a directory tree rooted at $out (e.g., pkgs.writeTextDir "etc/foo.conf" "..."). These are merged into the container root filesystem.

documents.title: Option: credentialsLeak.trivy.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable Trivy credentials leak scanning.

documents.title: Option: cve.grype.config.enabled
documents.content: Whether to use a grype config file.

documents.title: Option: cve.grype.config.path
documents.content: Path to the grype config file.

documents.title: Option: cve.grype.enabled
documents.content: Whether to run grype.

documents.title: Option: cve.rootPath
documents.content: The root path for CVE configuration files.

documents.title: Option: cve.trivy.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable Trivy CVE scanning.

documents.title: Option: cve.trivy.ignore.extra
documents.content: Extra CVEs to ignore.

documents.title: Option: cve.trivy.ignore.fileEnabled
documents.content: Whether to enable Enable trivy ignore file.

documents.title: Option: cve.trivy.ignore.path
documents.content: Path to the trivy ignore file.

documents.title: Option: cve.vulnix.enabled
documents.content: Whether to run vulnix CVE scanning.

documents.title: Option: cve.vulnix.whitelist.enabled
documents.content: Whether to use a vulnix whitelist file.

documents.title: Option: cve.vulnix.whitelist.path
documents.content: Path to the vulnix whitelist TOML file.

documents.title: Option: debug.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable debug build with additional debugging tools.

documents.title: Option: debug.entrypoint.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable debug entrypoint wrapper.

documents.title: Option: debug.entrypoint.wrapper
documents.content: Package containing the debug entrypoint wrapper.

documents.title: Option: debug.labels
documents.content: OCI image labels for the debug variant. Defaults to the production container's labels.

documents.title: Option: debug.packages
documents.content: List of additional packages to include in debug builds.

documents.title: Option: dependencies
documents.content: Additional dependencies packages to include in the container.

documents.title: Option: entrypoint
documents.content: The entrypoint command and arguments for the container. Will be automatically generated from the package if not specified.

documents.title: Option: fromImage.arch
documents.content: The architecture of the image.

documents.title: Option: fromImage.enabled
documents.content: Whether to use a base image for this container. Defaults to true when imageName is set.

documents.title: Option: fromImage.imageName
documents.content: The name of the base image.

documents.title: Option: fromImage.imageTag
documents.content: The tag/version of the image.

documents.title: Option: fromImage.os
documents.content: The operating system for the image.

documents.title: Option: homeConfig.enable
documents.content: Whether to enable home-manager dotfiles for the container user.

documents.title: Option: homeConfig.homeManagerFlake
documents.content: The home-manager flake input. Required when homeConfig.enable is true. Provides home-manager.nixosModules.home-manager for the NixOS eval. Example: homeConfig.homeManagerFlake = inputs.home-manager;

documents.title: Option: homeConfig.modules
documents.content: Home-manager modules for this container user's home directory. These configure dotfiles, shell, git, editor, etc. Requires homeConfig.homeManagerFlake to be set.

documents.title: Option: installNix
documents.content: Whether to install nix in the container.

documents.title: Option: isRoot
documents.content: Whether the container is a root container.

documents.title: Option: labels
documents.content:
OCI image labels (Config.Labels).
Key-value string pairs embedded in the image configuration. Keys should follow reverse domain notation (e.g. org.opencontainers.image.title).
These labels are set at image build time and are immutable once the image is produced.

documents.title: Option: multiArch.crossBuild.enable
Option: multiArch.enabled
documents.content: Whether multi-arch is enabled. Computed from multiArch.systems != [].

documents.title: Option: multiArch.systems
documents.content:
Target systems for multi-arch image building.
When non-empty, multi-arch is enabled and the listed systems define which architectures will be included in the manifest.
Supported: aarch64-linux, armv7l-linux, riscv64-linux, x86_64-linux
Uses Nix system strings (same convention as flake-parts systems).

documents.title: Option: multiArch.tempTagPrefix
documents.content: Prefix for temporary architecture-specific tags.

documents.title: Option: name
documents.content: Name of the container. If null, the name will be automatically generated from the package or base image.

documents.title: Option: nixosConfig.enable
documents.content: Whether to enable NixOS module-based configuration for this container.

documents.title: Option: nixosConfig.mainService
documents.content:
The NixOS service that this container runs.
When set, the container's package is auto-derived from the service module's package option, and an entrypoint wrapper script is generated from the service's systemd unit (preStart, ExecStartPre, ExecStart, directory creation).
Cannot be set together with package - they are mutually exclusive sources for the container's main program.
When null, nixosConfig only provides config files, users/groups, and extra packages (environment.systemPackages) as dependencies.

documents.title: Option: nixosConfig.modules
documents.content:
NixOS modules to evaluate for this container.
These modules are evaluated with boot.isContainer = true and the resulting config files, packages, users/groups, and entrypoint are extracted into the container image.
Only one service should be enabled per container (no init system). Use nixosConfig.mainService to designate which service this container runs.

documents.title: Option: optimizeLayers
documents.content:
Split container contents into multiple layers for better registry caching.
When enabled, dependencies are placed in a separate layer from the application package, and both are further split by popularity (inspired by Nixery/grahamc's layering strategy). This means:
Stable dependencies produce cached layers shared across rebuilds
Only the thin application layer is pushed on code changes

documents.title: Option: package
documents.content: The main package for the container

documents.title: Option: push
documents.content: Whether to push the container to the OCI registry.

documents.title: Option: registry
documents.content: Container registry prefix (e.g., "ghcr.io/my-org" or "my-registry.io/project"). If set, the full container name will be "registry/name". If null or empty string, no registry prefix will be added.

documents.title: Option: rootPath
documents.content: The root path for the container.

documents.title: Option: sbom.rootPath
documents.content: The root path for the SBOM.

documents.title: Option: sbom.syft.config.enabled
documents.content: Whether to use a syft config file.

documents.title: Option: sbom.syft.config.path
documents.content: Path to the syft config file.

documents.title: Option: sbom.syft.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable Syft SBOM generation.

documents.title: Option: signing.cosign.annotations
documents.content: Key-value annotations to attach to the cosign signature for this container.

documents.title: Option: signing.cosign.certificateIdentityRegexp
documents.content: Regexp to match certificate identity when verifying keyless signatures.

documents.title: Option: signing.cosign.certificateOidcIssuerRegexp
documents.content: Regexp to match OIDC issuer when verifying keyless signatures.

documents.title: Option: signing.cosign.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable cosign image signing for this container.

documents.title: Option: signing.cosign.key
documents.content: Path or KMS URI for cosign key for this container. Only used when keyless is false.

documents.title: Option: signing.cosign.keyless
documents.content: Whether to use keyless (OIDC) signing for this container.

documents.title: Option: signing.cosign.verify
documents.content: Whether to verify the signature immediately after signing.

documents.title: Option: tag
documents.content: Tag of the container.

documents.title: Option: tags
documents.content: List of tags for the container. All tags will be pushed to the registry. The first tag is used for the local build.

documents.title: Option: test.containerStructureTest.configs
documents.content: List of container-structure-test configuration files to run.

documents.title: Option: test.containerStructureTest.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable container-structure-test for validating container structure and metadata.

documents.title: Option: test.dgoss.command
documents.content: Override command to pass after the image reference when running dgoss run. Empty string (the default) lets the image's own entrypoint/cmd drive the container; in that mode the --entrypoint "" flag is NOT passed. Set this to e.g. "kubectl version" only if you explicitly want to override the entrypoint and your image ships that binary.

documents.title: Option: test.dgoss.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable dgoss testing for the container.

documents.title: Option: test.dgoss.hermetic
documents.content: Run dgoss as a pure Nix derivation (check) instead of a flake app. Uses podman inside the Nix sandbox. Requires extra-sandbox-paths = /sys/fs/cgroup in nix.conf.

documents.title: Option: test.dgoss.optionsPath
documents.content: Path to the dgoss configuration file.

documents.title: Option: test.dive.enabled
documents.content: Whether to enable Dive analysis for container image layers and efficiency.

documents.title: Option: test.rootPath
documents.content: The root path for the test.

documents.title: Option: user
documents.content: The user to run the container as. If null, will be automatically determined based on isRoot setting.

documents.title: Option: oci.backend
documents.content: Container runtime backend to load and run images.

documents.title: Option: oci.containers.<name>.autoStart
documents.content: When true, creates a runner service that starts the container after the image is loaded. The runner depends on the loader service.

documents.title: Option: oci.containers.<name>.dependencies
documents.content: Additional packages included in the container root filesystem.

documents.title: Option: oci.containers.<name>.entrypoint
documents.content: OCI entrypoint (command + arguments). When empty, defaults to the package's main program.

documents.title: Option: oci.containers.<name>.environment
documents.content: Environment variables passed to the container at runtime.

documents.title: Option: oci.containers.<name>.image
documents.content: Built OCI image (computed from package + dependencies via nix2container).

documents.title: Option: oci.containers.<name>.isRoot
documents.content: Whether the container process runs as root.

documents.title: Option: oci.containers.<name>.name
documents.content: OCI image name. Defaults to the container attribute name.

documents.title: Option: oci.containers.<name>.optimizeLayers
documents.content: Split container into multiple layers for better local caching. Defaults to true for deploy (no push cost, shared layers save disk).

documents.title: Option: oci.containers.<name>.package
documents.content: The main application package to containerize.

documents.title: Option: oci.containers.<name>.ports
documents.content: Port mappings for the container runner (e.g. ["8080:8080"]). Only used when autoStart = true.

documents.title: Option: oci.containers.<name>.tag
documents.content: OCI image tag.

documents.title: Option: oci.containers.<name>.user
documents.content: User to run the container process as.

documents.title: Option: oci.containers.<name>.volumes
documents.content: Volume mounts for the container runner (e.g. ["/data:/data"]). Only used when autoStart = true.

documents.title: Option: oci.enable
documents.content: Whether to enable nix-oci container deployment.

documents.title: Option: oci.container.configFiles
documents.content: Additional config file derivations for the root filesystem.

documents.title: Option: oci.container.dependencies
documents.content: Additional packages to include in the root filesystem.

documents.title: Option: oci.container.entrypoint
documents.content: Container entrypoint. Auto-derived from mainService when set.

documents.title: Option: oci.container.gid
documents.content: GID for non-root container user.

documents.title: Option: oci.container.isRoot
documents.content: Whether the container runs as root.

documents.title: Option: oci.container.mainService
documents.content: NixOS service to extract entrypoint from.

documents.title: Option: oci.container.package
documents.content: Main package for the container.

documents.title: Option: oci.container.uid
documents.content: UID for non-root container user.

documents.title: Option: oci.container.user
documents.content: Container user name.
